Pruned 761 lines of dead orchestration layer + dead endpoints + legacy constants. Shipped on dev in 2452130.

Apr 2026 audit complete. Findings: ~700 line dead orchestration layer, dead routes (5), dead frontend exports (4), dead constants (2). All shipped in 2452130. Layer separation extracts (2 violations) deferred to next cycle.
v73 migration deletes orphan MiLB league rows (a, aa, aaa, higha) and remaps user-data references to new codes; tests added; verified on copy of prod DB

Shipped on dev: Jellyfin integration parallel to Emby. Refactored EmbyClient with PATH_PREFIX (Emby keeps /emby/, Jellyfin uses server root). Added schema columns, settings dataclass, read/update helpers, Pydantic models, /settings/jellyfin + /jellyfin/test routes, refresh hook in generation.py. Frontend: combined Emby and Jellyfin into a single 'Media Servers' tab. Visual verification via Playwright. 4 jellyfin client tests added (518 total pass).

Root cause: stale stream_match_cache rows had cached_event_data with home_team.name set but home_team.short_name=''. Diagnosed from user @millercentral's EPG XML (#201): {away_team_short} at {home_team_short} → '<display-name>at</display-name>', and {broadcast_feed_team} fell back to full team name on home feed ('Philadelphia Phillies Feed') because feed_team.short_name was empty. Fix: TeamMatcher._reconstruct_event now treats a row with name set but short_name empty as stale, returns None, deletes the cache row, and re-fetches via service.get_events on next match pass. Self-healing — no user action required. 4 tests added (522 total pass).

Real fix on dev (3 layers): (1) refresh_event_status is now additive — overlays only mutable fields (status, scores, broadcasts, odds, fight_result_*) onto the original event. Teams and other static fields are preserved verbatim. ESPN's summary endpoint omits shortDisplayName, so the old code was wiping team.short_name=None over the populated scoreboard data on every refresh. (2) Service-layer backfill from team_cache: get_event/get_events/get_team_schedule walk teams and patch short_name/abbreviation/name from team_cache when missing. team_cache (refreshed on startup from /teams endpoints) is now the canonical identity source, decoupled from per-event endpoint shape. (3) ESPN _parse_team falls back to team.name (the short form, always populated) when shortDisplayName is missing — defense in depth for new endpoints. 17 new tests, 548 total pass.

Fixed on dev 3050ac7: removed dead eventsround.php, repointed sparse-league fallback to eventsseason.php (gated to SEASON_FALLBACK_LEAGUES), fail-fast on 404s. 607 tests pass (+3 regression). Unrivaled preserved. GH #217 commented; close issue at v2.5.7 release.
GO. Endpoint live-validated; data-shape doc + go/no-go delivered. Unblocks 183.2.

Added EPGManager.search_programs() + supports_program_search() feature-detection + DispatcharrProgram dataclass. Live-verified on Dispatcharr 0.24.0. 11 unit tests, full suite green (618), ruff clean. Architecture docs + version dependency documented.

Added EPGProgramIndex (consumers/matching/epg_index.py): scoped per-run fetch (one call per distinct event-group tvg_id, NOT whole instance), _Teamarr exclusion, tvg_id index + half-open overlap lookup. Added categories to DispatcharrProgram (custom_properties.categories). 10 index tests + dataclass test; full suite 628 green; ruff clean. Architecture docs updated.

EPG-title matching capability complete. StreamMatcher consumes injected EPGProgramIndex: title+sub_title through shared classify_stream->TeamMatcher (extracted _route_to_outcomes, reused by name+EPG paths), category gating (classic>non-event>event precedence, absent=attempt+fallback), one-stream-many-events fan-out, MatchMethod.EPG + program windows threaded through MatchOutcome/MatchedStreamResult, _reconcile_epg (linear=EPG wins, dedicated=name wins). Reuses TeamMatcher cache (free per-program memoization) — dropped redundant compute_program_fingerprint. 25 EPG tests, full suite 653 green, ruff clean, docs updated. Live wiring (build+inject index for opted-in groups) is 183.6.

Time-windowed stream membership complete. Window columns + gate model (user-confirmed): attach_at/detach_at on managed_channel_streams (NULL=full-life). compute_stream_window() = EPG program slot +/- global buffer, CLIPPED to neighbour programs (back-to-back wrong-game avoidance). get_ordered_stream_ids() window-aware (active=removed_at NULL AND (attach_at NULL OR attach_at<=now<detach_at)); reconciliation drift uses window-gated 'expected'. epg_program_start/end + clip bounds threaded match->lifecycle. epg_stream_pre/post_buffer settings. removed_at stays terminal. 20 windowing/clip tests, full suite 669 green, docs updated. Default-off until 183.6 wires EPG matching. Ordering predicate split to 183.8.

Reported Spanish categories are all live-event labels that already pass the gate (ATTEMPT); the only behavior-changing fix (skipping non-English replays/non-events) needs real Spanish CLASSIC/NON_EVENT category strings we don't have and likely won't get. Gate fails safe today. Reopen if a replay-labeled non-English category surfaces from an actual guide.

Root cause: ESPN.supports_league() dotted-soccer discovery heuristic ('.' in league -> True) ran before honoring the DB provider assignment, so ESPN (priority 0) claimed uru.2 at fetch time despite provider=tsdb. Fix: decline a league explicitly mapped to a different provider in the leagues table before the dot fallback; discovered leagues (not in table) still resolve via the heuristic. 6 regression tests.

Closed as REDUNDANT — already covered by the existing epg_match ordering rule. Goal was to let users sort time-shared linear streams distinctly from dedicated streams. Verified at matcher.py:668-670 that every MatchMethod.EPG assignment ALSO sets epg_program_start/end -> attach_at/detach_at, so match_method='epg' <=> time-windowed linear, always (name matches are the full-life ones). The epg_match rule (stream_ordering.py:251-257) keys on match_method=='epg', which therefore already selects exactly the time-shared linear streams — surfaced in the UI as the 'EPG matched stream' Stream Type option (djl). The proposed attach_at-based discriminator would split EPG matches into windowed vs non-windowed, but no non-windowed EPG matches exist in practice, so it adds no real capability. Reopen only if a future path produces full-life EPG matches.

Shipped in v2.6.0. Global master toggle epg_match_enabled (default OFF, opt-in) + attach/detach buffers epg_stream_pre_buffer_minutes/epg_stream_post_buffer_minutes (default 60) live across backend (settings types/read/update/routes/models) and surfaced in Settings -> EPG; per-group epg_match_enabled toggle in EventGroupForm + bulk EventGroupImport. Feature-detected on Dispatcharr 0.24.0 (graceful off on older builds). Design decisions resolved: GLOBAL toggle + global buffers + per-group enable (not per-sport); window source resolved to always use the EPG program start/end window (no separate setting — that's the purpose of EPG matching). Covered by test_settings_epg_route_parity; documented in docs/guide/settings/epg.md + guide/epg-matching.md.

Tests + docs complete. Coverage audit: search_programs success + 404 feature-detect, bulk index + half-open overlap lookup, one-stream->many-events fan-out with per-program windows, dedicated full-life path unchanged — all covered. Closed the one PARTIAL gap (checklist #5) with test_same_stream_rotates_across_channels_one_day: a single physical stream rotating across 3 event channels in a day (attach->detach->re-attach, incl. a midnight-spanning window). Docs comprehensive (guide/epg-matching.md: opt-in default, buffers, Dispatcharr 0.24.0 + feature-detect, cadence-granularity caveat, ordering rule; settings/epg.md + settings/channels.md cross-linked; CLAUDE.md architecture note present); bumped guide docs_version 2.5.7->2.6.0. Note: bead's 'back-to-back buffer clipping' test item is obsolete — removed in 2.6.0 (6qx/e89c966); a test asserts the attribute no longer exists. 763 tests pass.

Shipped in v2.6.0 (commit 6d8363d). Both children complete: 183.10 (global 'Use Dispatcharr channels as EPG source' toggle in Settings -> EPG) + 183.11 (backend candidate filter to Dispatcharr-mapped streams, hidden system group, dedupes against existing EPG-match groups). Stale-open epic; closing now that 2/2 children are done and the feature is in the released product.

Epic complete — EPG Program-Data Matching shipped in v2.6.0. All 13 children closed: client+feature-detect (183.2), bulk index+overlap (183.3), matcher one-stream->many-events (183.4), time-windowed membership (183.5), settings/UI toggle+buffers (183.6), tests+docs (183.7), ordering predicate redundant/closed (183.8), Dispatcharr-channels EPG source sub-epic (183.9), plus spikes/bugfixes (183.1/183.12/4c9/6z1/npz).

Removed the V1-parity leftover db_updates['dispatcharr_stream_id']=stream_id at service.py:1743 — managed_channels has no such column (it's on managed_channel_streams; DB membership is written by add_stream_to_channel). The Dispatcharr-side drift fix (update_data['streams']) remains. +3 regression tests (test_managed_channel_update_columns.py) pinning the schema split. 766 tests pass.

Added epg_xtream_cache_hours (int, default 24) across schema.sql, settings types/models/read/update, /settings/epg route + combined /settings route; threaded through event_group_processor._build_epg_index -> _add_xtream_epg_fallback -> fetch_xtream_programs(ttl_seconds=hours*3600), replacing the hardcoded 6h. Frontend: reveal 'Cache for (hours)' input under the XC fallback toggle. Docs: guide/epg-matching.md + settings/epg.md. 766 tests pass, frontend build OK.

Added epg_channel_source_groups setting (JSON list of DP channel-group ids, default []=all) across schema/types/models/read/update + dedicated & combined /settings routes (update.py now JSON-encodes list/dict values). _fetch_channel_source_streams now scopes candidates to the selected DP channel groups (ch.channel_group_id), checked early to skip EPG-resolution/matching for undesired groups (the perf win); each candidate also stashes dp_channel_group_id/dp_channel_group for the sorting rule (ybt.3). Reused existing GET /dispatcharr/channel-groups for the picker (no new endpoint). +2 tests. 768 pass. UI picker + docs land in ybt.4.

Dispatcharr Group ordering rule. Backend: persist dispatcharr_channel_group on managed_channel_streams (schema col + add_stream_to_channel allowlist + ManagedChannelStream + 3 call sites thread stream.dp_channel_group / preserved on keyword moves); 'dispatcharr_group' added to VALID_RULE_TYPES (value-required); _match_dispatcharr_group (case-insensitive, NULL never matches) + dispatch. Frontend: rule type in RULE_TYPES + unions, value <Select> populated from selected DP groups resolved to names (getSettings + getDispatcharrChannelGroups), RichTooltip helper mirroring home/away. +3 ordering tests. 771 pass, build OK. Docs: channels.md + epg-matching.md.

Cascading DP-group multi-select under 'Use Dispatcharr channels as an EPG source'. Reused existing channelGroupsQuery (/dispatcharr/channel-groups) + CheckboxListPicker (ids<->names), saving to epg_channel_source_groups via full-object updateEPG. Helper text explains perf scoping + the sort-rule link. Docs: settings/epg.md row + guide channel-source section. Build OK.

Both tweaks shipped to dev. (1) Configurable XC EPG cache TTL (ybt.1). (2) DP channel-source group scoping + Dispatcharr Group sorting rule (ybt.2/3/4): select which DP channel groups to scan (perf win) and sort by them. 771 tests, ruff clean, frontend builds.

Null-guarded the localeCompare/toLowerCase sorts that blanked Settings when a cached league had a null name/sport: filteredLeagues memo (Settings.tsx) sort + search, StreamOrderingManager team sort, and the shared sortLeaguesImportFirst helper (utils.ts). Coalesce to '' so one null-named row can't crash the page. Frontend builds. Root data cause (why a league/team name is null) left as a separate backend follow-up.

dynamic_resolver tracks valid Dispatcharr group ids at init (_known_group_ids, trusted only when the list_groups fetch succeeded); _validate_group_id drops a configured static/per-league group id that no longer exists to ungrouped (None) with a clear warning, so a deleted Dispatcharr group can't fail every channel creation (0 channels). +4 tests. 775 pass.

Live test-fetch validator: lookupleague(id) resolves identity, validate_tsdb_sport_matches cross-checks sport, eventsnextleague(id) returns sample events. Premium-gated (403). Wired into create_custom_league as the authoritative server-side guardrail — blocks save on zero events with allow_empty override for off-season. name_matches flag (typed name vs canonical strLeague) + resolved_via surface the id-vs-name debugging the bead wanted. SCOPING: did not add a literal eventsday-by-name fallback path; the id-based path is reliable and name_matches covers the debugging need — can be a follow-up if real id/name confusion shows up. Raw TSDB client methods (lookup_league_raw/get_events_by_date_raw/get_next_events_raw) added uncached. Tests cover gate, unresolvable id, sport mismatch, empty-ok, key passthrough, create guardrail + override + cross-check.

Custom Leagues UI: dedicated page (route + Event Groups entry button, mirrors Detection Library). Premium-gated (locked card when capability.enabled=false). List table of is_custom=1 leagues with edit/delete. Add/edit dialog: code/id/name/display + sport dropdown (functional sports from capability) + event_type + tsdb_tier; league_code immutable on edit. Test-Fetch UX shows live TSDB league name/sport/sample events + name_matches + off-season warning before save; allow_empty override checkbox. Added GET /leagues/custom list endpoint + list_custom_leagues DB helper. Playwright-verified end-to-end against real backend + live TSDB: empty state, dropdown, gating, bad-id 400 toast, good-id (EPL 4328) result panel, full create->list->delete cycle (DB left clean). Frontend build clean. NOTE: premium-gate locked state not visually exercised (dev DB has a key) — covered by conditional + backend capability test. User-facing docs deferred to eqz.6.

Auto team-cache refresh on custom-league create. Verification caught the core bug: create committed the row but never reloaded the in-memory league mapping, so the TSDB provider couldn't resolve the new code and cached 0 teams while reporting success. Fix: reload mapping after create+commit, before refresh; plus supports_league guard so an unresolvable league reports success=false not a silent zero. Verified live: custom league -> 16 Superettan teams cached. Filed eqz.9 for delete-orphan asymmetry.
Added italic footer line in MainLayout.tsx; verified rendering via Playwright

Default-output live detection (recognize 'sports' category, report is_live from actual <live> tag) + moved inline xmltv query to database.teams.get_team_xmltv. Fixed date-fragile endpoint tests (now use live-now fixtures). 851 tests pass.

Removed global epg_match_enabled switch. EPG matching always available; sole control is per-group epg_match_enabled. Removed global gate from consumer (system-group sync, index gate, dedup path), settings plumbing (types/read/update/models/routes), and frontend toggle (un-nested buffers/channel-source/Xtream). DB column left vestigial. Docs updated (epg-matching.md, settings/epg.md, CLAUDE.md). 851 tests pass, frontend builds, verified in browser + PUT round-trip.

Added global epg_channel_source_mode ('streams'|'channels'). 'channels' emits one representative stream (lowest id) per DP channel in _fetch_channel_source_streams so its lineup isn't fragmented; 'streams' keeps current per-stream behavior. Cascading menu under 'Use Dispatcharr as a source'. Schema column (reconciled), full settings plumbing, 2 new tests, docs. Verified in browser + API round-trip.
EPG source/matching UX restructure complete: 3lp1.1 (removed global EPG switch, per-group control) + 3lp1.2 (process-as-channels mode, cascading menu).

Moved EPG program-data matching settings from Settings→EPG (Output Settings card) to Settings→Event Groups as its own 'EPG Program Matching' Card, between Event Matching and Exception Keywords. Frontend-only move (verified in browser, no duplication, EPG tab clean). Docs relocated: section moved epg.md->event-groups.md with pointer, guide location refs updated.

Added v74 migration _migrate_v74_preserve_epg_match_offstate: if old vestigial global epg_match_enabled was OFF, clear epg_channel_source_enabled + per-group epg_match_enabled to preserve off-state across the global-switch removal. Verified on dev DB (global was ON -> correctly no-op). 856 tests pass.

VERIFIED WORKING end-to-end against live CDVR v2026.05.18 (192.168.0.10:8089) on 2026-06-14. Both step-5d branches confirmed via CDVR's own /log (Fetched+Indexed, not just 200): (a) M3U refresh -> success; (b) explicit-lineup EPG PUT -> 'Fetched guide data for XMLTV-dispatcharr in 2s' + 'Indexed 34868 airings' @13:31; (c) DERIVED-lineup branch (the actual bug fix: lineup unset -> derives XMLTV-<source>) -> derives 'XMLTV-dispatcharr', lineup_derived=True, fires, 'Indexed 34867 airings' @13:34. test_connection/list_m3u_sources/list_lineups all succeed. Audited all hand-rolled controls in the builder; converted the one clean win: XmltvTab raw <select> -> Select primitive. Rejected false-positives with reasoning: (1) BasicTab duration radios are app-standard (raw radios used across EventGroups/Teams/GlobalDefaults; RadioCards is for bordered-card selectors and would degrade this compact list w/ its inline custom-hours input); (2) DefaultsTab per-item collapsible is a list-item disclosure w/ shared expand-state + delete actions — converting risks default-expand behavior change, not a clean CollapsibleSection (single-section) fit; (3) VariableSidebar <details> deferred to .4 (sidebar rework, noted). tsc+build+lint green.

Scope gating audited end-to-end and found ESSENTIALLY CORRECT — no retags warranted. The subagent audit flagged ~16 'team-perspective vars offered to events that resolve empty', but its core PREMISE was wrong: event templates build context in HOME-TEAM PERSPECTIVE (event_epg.py:195 team_id=event.home_team.id; context_builder.build_for_event fetches team_stats for that team + opponent_stats for the away team). So home_record/away_record/home_win_pct/away_win_pct (read ctx.team_stats = home team's splits) and the positional home_team_*/away_team_* families DO resolve in event templates. Verified in code; reverted an initial (wrong) TEAM_ONLY retag after maintainer correctly pointed out home/away team data resolves in event context. End-to-end gating IS wired (GET /variables?template_type= -> filter_by_template_type; TemplateForm passes type). Dead VariablePicker.tsx (zero importers) is ungated but harmless. One real non-scope bug spun out: 'matchup' name collision (combat MMA variant shadowed by identity). Net: leave scopes as-is.

Investigated all hardcoded colors. The only genuinely off-brand one was the blue event-type badge+banner (bg-blue-500/20 text-blue-400) — already fixed in .9 (now semantic bg-primary/15 text-primary + bg-secondary banner). The rest are NOT violations: (1) TemplateField + ConditionsTab amber are the app-wide WARNING convention — amber-400/500 used for warnings across EventGroups/StatusStrip/Settings/GlobalDefaults/EventGroupForm; keeping them is consistent. (2) VariableSidebar blue/gray/green/red is an INTENTIONAL educational suffix-type legend (ALL/BASE/.next/.last) — flattening to semantic tokens would lose the colour-coded meaning; left as-is (revisit holistically in .4 if desired). No further code changes needed.

Templates.tsx list page swept. Page was already largely consistent (Table primitive, Badge variants, Dialog/Alert primitives, icon-only ghost row actions w/ tooltips = app-standard, Loader2 spinner). Fixes: (1) error-state h1 text-2xl -> text-xl (matched normal state + app); (2) type Badge now shows Tv/User icon + capitalized label, matching the builder's type indicators for cross-surface consistency. Empty state is plain text (no EmptyState primitive exists yet — 1kt3.9). Verified live via Playwright. tsc+build green; no new lint (pre-existing line-178 unused-vars only).

Audited SAMPLE_DATA vs registry: 202 base vars, 10 had NO sample (feed_team/feed_team_short/feed_team_abbrev/_lower/feed_team_logo/is_home_feed/is_away_feed/feed_home_away/broadcast_feed/broadcast_feed_team — the event-only feed-separation family) so the preview showed raw {name}. Added realistic per-sport samples (models channel carrying the HOME team's feed; mirrors home_team values, is_home_feed=true). All 202 now covered; get_all_sample_data resolves for every AVAILABLE_SPORT (fallback verified). Added tests/test_sample_data_coverage.py (2 tests) as a regression guard. ruff + tests green. Unblocks .6 (live gamethumb render).

Added live image rendering to the preview. New ImagePreview in TemplateField fetches+renders the resolved URL as an with loading (spinner) + broken-link ('Image didn't load — check the URL resolves') states. Wired via isImageField on the 5 art/image fields: program_art_url, event_channel_logo_url, pregame/postgame/idle art_url. Only renders for absolute http(s) URLs (relative paths await the art base URL feature z02s). Verified live via Playwright: valid URLs load (naturalWidth>0), an invalid URL shows the broken-link message. This is the highest-signal preview validation — proves a template produces a working thumbnail end-to-end. tsc+build+lint green.
schema art_base_url (v75) + EPGSettings type/read/update + combined & dedicated API endpoints. Verified live.

apply_art_base_url in xmltv.py prefixes relative icons at serialization (absolute passthrough); wired at both programmes_to_xmltv call sites (team_processor, event_group_processor). Frontend preview mirrors it. Verified live.
'Game-Thumbs Base URL' as its own tile in EPG Output (per maintainer), links github.com/sethwv/game-thumbs. Verified live.
Documented base URL setting + relative-path convention + upgrade behavior in docs/guide/game-thumbs.md.

v75 migration deduces most-frequent art origin -> base URL + relativizes matching template art (leading slash); v76 normalizes existing relative paths to leading slash; create/update_template normalize on write. 12 tests. Verified: dev DB migrated to art_base_url=sportslogos.jesmann.com, templates relative w/ leading slash.

Game-thumbs base URL feature complete + verified live on event template edit: base URL setting (own tile), relative art paths, XMLTV prefixing, live preview base-application (debounced, fixed box, broken-URL indicator), deduce+normalize migrations, create/edit write normalization. Full suite 915 green; tsc+build+ruff clean.

Fixed via persistKey on the alias + keyword CollapsibleSections (detlib-team_aliases / detlib-<category>). usePersistentCollapse restores expanded state from localStorage across the inline-component remount. tsc+build green.

Unified art-URL resolver: base reconstruction centralized in TemplateResolver.resolve_art() + shared teamarr/utilities/art_url.py, injected via DI into all 5 resolver sites. Every art sink (EPG icon, DP channel logo, fillers) now emits the reconstructed URL identically. Port preserved (never stripped/hardcoded); absolute URLs pass through idempotently. 916 tests green; ruff clean.
